key_departures.impact: Starting left guard signed a 4-year, $61M deal with New Orleans. Buffalo kept center Connor McGovern but could not retain both interior starters. Alec Anderson (backup) is the in-house fallback at LG, creating a clear starter-level hole.

Starting nickel cornerback traded to Raiders for a 6th-round pick (Bills sent a 7th). Johnson was the team's best slot defender and was set to earn $8.5M+ in 2026. Departure creates a direct nickel CB need.
Veteran backup QB departed on a 2-year deal with Tennessee. Leaves the QB2 role vacant behind Josh Allen.
Starting fullback departed on a 2-year deal worth up to $12M ($6M gtd) with New England. Niche but real hole in Buffalo's run-heavy offensive scheme.
Released after failing a physical. Was a starting safety. Combined with Darnell Savage also departing (1-year deal to Pittsburgh Steelers), the Bills lost two safeties from their depth chart.
Signed a 1-year deal with Pittsburgh. Secondary depth loss compounds the Taylor Rapp release. Cole Bishop is the only clear returning starter at safety.
Released for cap savings. Was a veteran slot/depth receiver. Adds modest pressure to the WR depth chart, but WR need was already pre-existing.
Released for cap savings. Was a depth/rotational corner. Loss is manageable given low role.
Signed a 2-year deal with the Bears. Depth defensive back loss.
1-year deal expired. Led Bills with 5 sacks + a league-leading 5 forced fumbles in 2025. Departure left EDGE as the team's most urgent defensive need entering free agency.
Contract expired. Rotational edge rusher departed, compounding the pass rush need.

contract.notes: Existing Bears extension (4yr/$110M signed 2024) assumed in full. 2026 salary $23.485M fully guaranteed; 2027 salary becomes fully guaranteed March 13, 2026; Bills also guaranteed $15.5M of 2028 salary as part of trade. Bills paid 2026 2nd-round pick (#60) and received CHI 2026 5th-round pick (#165). Contract being restructured to lower 2026 cap hit to ~$7.5M. Guarantees TBD on full 2029 year.

signings.analysis: The biggest offensive move of Buffalo's offseason. Moore is a proven WR1 with nine straight NFL seasons of fully guaranteed compensation and a reunion with HC Joe Brady, under whom he had his two best statistical seasons in Carolina. Trade cost was a 2026 2nd-round pick (#60), a significant investment. Buffalo's WR room generated the 9th-fewest receiving yards in the NFL in 2025. Moore is the clear WR1 alongside Khalil Shakir, and at $23.5M/year he is legitimately elite AAV-tier. The 4-year structure with multi-year guarantees signals a long-term commitment. WR drops from a Tier 1 need to a Tier 3-4 depth/developmental consideration.

contract.notes: Max value $52.5M with incentives. Per Spotrac/OverTheCap confirmed.

signings.analysis: The most critical defensive signing of the offseason. Chubb had 8.5 sacks in 2025 and would have led the entire Bills roster if on the team a year earlier. At $14.5M AAV, he lands in low-end starter territory for EDGE (market: $15-25M is starter tier), aided by the fact he's already earned $98M in career earnings and accepted a modestly-priced deal. The 3-year, $29M guaranteed structure is a real investment but not top-of-market. Key injury flags: he's torn the ACL in both knees, missed all of 2024. He slots in as the starter opposite Greg Rousseau in Jim Leonhard's new 3-4. EDGE drops from Tier 1 (critical) to Tier 2-3 — still a need for developmental depth given health concerns and aging curve, but no longer urgent. Rating: Partially addressed rather than fully, due to injury history and below-elite AAV on a 3-year deal.

contract.notes: 4yr/$52M with $32M guaranteed. Per ESPN/Spotrac confirmed. AAV is 3rd-4th highest among all NFL centers.

signings.analysis: Locking up the starting center was essential; McGovern has started all 49 regular-season games since joining in 2023 and posted a 97.2% pass-block win rate in 2025. The 4-year, $32M guaranteed deal is a real commitment at C. However, by retaining McGovern, the Bills could not also keep starting LG David Edwards (who signed a 4yr/$61M deal in New Orleans). The IOL need shifts from 'C AND LG' to just 'LG/interior depth' — Alec Anderson is the fallback at LG on a 1-year prove-it deal. IOL partially addressed: C is locked up long-term but LG is an active starter-level need.

contract.notes: Base deal 3yr/$15.75M, max $21M with incentives, $7.875M fully guaranteed at signing (OTC). $4.5M signing bonus. 2027 salary partially guaranteed, becomes fully guaranteed early in 2027 league year. Per OverTheCap and Spotrac confirmed.

signings.analysis: Alford is the direct replacement for traded nickel CB Taron Johnson. He spent the majority of his 4-year Atlanta career operating from the slot (64 games, 23 starts), posting career highs across the board in 2025: 3 INTs, 2 sacks, 6 TFLs. At $5.25M AAV, this is depth-market money for CB (starter tier is $12-20M), though the 3-year structure and $10.12M in total guarantees signal the Bills see him as more than a stopgap. He fills the nickel role in Leonhard's new defense. CB need is partially addressed for nickel specifically — outside CB depth (following Dane Jackson's release and Tre'Davious White's departure) is still a real concern for the draft.

contract.notes: New 3-year deal through 2028 (restructured from bloated $17.1M 2026 cap hit). Total value and full guarantee structure TBD per ESPN/Tom Pelissero — cap hit in 2026 expected to drop $3-6M from $17.1M. AAV estimated at ~$7M based on reporting. Guarantees TBD from final paperwork.

signings.analysis: Re-signing Knox was primarily a cap restructure — his previous $17.1M cap hit was unsustainable, and the new 3-year deal lowers his 2026 number by an estimated $3-6M. He remains the starting TE alongside 2023 1st-round pick Dalton Kincaid and 2025 5th-round pick Jackson Hawes. This is one of the NFL's better TE rooms. Knox had 417 yards and 4 TDs in 2025. The position is fully addressed with three capable contributors; no TE investment needed in the draft.

contract.notes: 2yr/$4.1M base, max $6.1M with incentives. $300K signing bonus, $1.65M base 2026 ($1.5M gtd), $1.95M base 2027. Per Spotrac confirmed.

signings.analysis: Allen is a low-cost veteran insurance policy behind Josh Allen, who has not missed a start since his rookie season (122 consecutive). The 2-year, $4.1M base deal costs Buffalo essentially nothing against the cap. Allen knows the system (2023 stint in BUF), is close friends with the starter, and stepped in admirably as backup in Detroit. QB2 need is resolved at an appropriate cost for this roster construction.

contract.notes: 3yr/$6.49M, max $7.5M. $1.25M signing bonus, $2.53M fully guaranteed at signing. Per OverTheCap/Buffalo Rumblings confirmed.

signings.analysis: Franklin is a special teams ace who logged 329 of 335 snaps on special teams in 2025. He has only 9 career defensive starts. Retaining him on a cap-friendly 3-year deal adds special teams continuity, but does nothing to address the starter-level S vacancy left by the releases of Taylor Rapp and Darnell Savage's departure. Cole Bishop is the lone returning starter at safety. Buffalo still urgently needs a second safety starter either in free agency or in the draft.

contract.notes: 1yr/$2.5M, $500K signing bonus, $1.8M guaranteed. Per Spotrac confirmed.

signings.analysis: Anderson is the in-house fallback at LG after David Edwards' departure to New Orleans. He can play all five OL positions and has appeared in 17 games. His 1-year deal signals the Bills view him as bridge/competition, not a solved problem at LG. The IOL starter void at LG remains — Anderson is a depth answer, not a long-term solution. Bills will likely look for a LG upgrade in the draft.
Former 2nd-round pick retained on a near-minimum deal. Provides depth at DT alongside Ed Oliver. No meaningful need impact — this is a camp body/depth signing at minimum cost.

contract.notes: 1-year veteran minimum deal per The Athletic/Buffalo News. Exact value approximately vet minimum (~$1.215M for his experience level).

signings.analysis: Wishnowsky stabilized the punting position after a revolving door through the first four weeks of 2025. His 41.8 net yards/punt ranked 2nd in his career and he led the NFL in limiting punt returns. Veteran minimum deal is outstanding value. Punter need is fully resolved at essentially no cap cost.

contract.notes: 1yr/$1.15M reserve/future contract. Per Buffalo Rumblings confirmed.

signings.analysis: Third QB on depth chart behind both Allens. Reserve/futures deal. Camp body at QB with no meaningful need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: DJ Moore trade (4yr/$110M inherited, 2026+2027 salaries fully guaranteed, cost a 2026 2nd-round pick) gives Buffalo a true WR1. Moore is a proven 1,000-yard receiver who excelled under new HC Joe Brady in Carolina. WR is no longer a premium need — depth/developmental WR in rounds 4-7 is appropriate, but don't reach early.

Bradley Chubb signing (3yr/$43.5M, $29M gtd) is a legitimate starter-level addition opposite Greg Rousseau. Chubb posted 8.5 sacks in 2025 and would have led the Bills roster. However, ACL injuries in both knees and below-elite AAV ($14.5M) temper the certainty. EDGE drops from critical (Tier 1) but remains a Tier 2 need — the Bills should still target a developmental EDGE in rounds 2-3 given health risk, and depth remains thin (Hoecht/Jackson both recovering from season-ending injuries).

Re-signing McGovern (4yr/$52M, $32M gtd) solves center long-term. But losing starting LG David Edwards (4yr/$61M to Saints) created an equivalent hole. Alec Anderson (1yr/$2.5M) is a rotational bridge at LG, not a solved starter. IOL need remains Tier 2 but shifts from 'C or LG' entirely to 'LG starter' — this is a priority Round 2-3 draft target.

Dee Alford (3yr/$15.75M, $10.12M gtd) replaces traded nickel CB Taron Johnson at slot cornerback — same role, lower price, less proven. Outside CB depth is thin: Christian Benford and Maxwell Hairston are the starters, but Dane Jackson was released and Tre'Davious White is a UFA. CB remains Tier 2 — Alford handles the nickel need adequately but outside CB depth needs addressing in the draft (rounds 2-4).

Safety escalated to Tier 1 need. Taylor Rapp (released/failed physical) and Darnell Savage Jr. (signed with Pittsburgh) both departed. Cole Bishop is the lone returning starter. Sam Franklin Jr. re-signed (3yr/$6.49M) but is purely a special teams player (329 of 335 snaps on ST in 2025). The Bills have a legitimate starter vacancy opposite Bishop. Safety should be addressed in the first three rounds of the draft or in ongoing free agency.

Jim Leonhard's new 3-4 scheme requires off-ball LBs who fit his vision. Matt Milano (UFA, unsigned), Shaq Thompson (UFA, unsigned), and Baylon Spector (UFA, unsigned) are all available but unsigned — no Bills LB signings have been made. LB remains a Tier 2 need with the scheme change amplifying positional importance. Draft priority rounds 2-4.

Ed Oliver returns and Phidarian Mathis (1yr minimum) re-signed. No major IDL losses or additions. DT depth (Jones, Ogunjobi, Phillips all UFA and unsigned) is thin, but Oliver anchors the unit. IDL stays Tier 3 — draft depth in rounds 4-6.

Knox re-extension (3yr through 2028) locks up TE1 alongside Dalton Kincaid and Jackson Hawes. One of the NFL's deepest TE rooms. TE is effectively off the board — skip in draft entirely unless elite value falls.

key_additions.headline: Bills' biggest need solved: proven WR1 reunites with HC Joe Brady, costs 2026 2nd-round pick (#60)
Two-time Pro Bowl pass rusher (8.5 sacks in 2025) fills urgent EDGE need opposite Greg Rousseau
Starting center re-signed on long-term deal; started all 49 BUF regular-season games since 2023
Nickel CB replaces traded Taron Johnson; career year in 2025 with 3 INTs, 2 sacks
TE re-extension through 2028 preserves Josh Allen's trusted target; cap-friendly restructure saves $3-6M in 2026
Josh Allen's close friend returns as backup QB, replacing departing Mitch Trubisky
Special teams ace re-signed on 3-year deal; played 329 of 335 snaps on ST in 2025

key_losses.headline: Starting LG departs on massive deal to New Orleans; leaves starter-level void at left guard

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to LV Raiders (Bills received 6th-round pick, sent 7th)
key_losses.headline: Starter nickel CB traded away in draft pick swap; Dee Alford signed as replacement
Bills' 2025 sack leader (5 sacks, 5 forced fumbles) departs; Bradley Chubb signed as replacement
Starting safety released after failing physical; leaves significant hole opposite Cole Bishop
Backup QB departs for Tennessee; Kyle Allen signed as direct replacement
Starting fullback and key run-game piece departs to AFC East rival New England
Depth safety departs to Pittsburgh, compounding Bills' urgent need for a starter opposite Cole Bishop

notes: All contract figures sourced from Spotrac, OverTheCap, ESPN, NFL.com, and official Bills announcements. DJ Moore's full 2026 cap hit will be restructured upon trade processing (reported ~$7.5M post-restructure vs. $24.5M base). Dawson Knox's exact new contract total and full guarantee breakdown have not been officially disclosed as of March 13, 2026 — 3-year structure through 2028 confirmed, AAV and guarantees estimated from reporting. Alec Anderson's Spotrac-listed figure ($2.5M) differs slightly from the Ian Rapoport-reported $3M figure; $2.5M used as Spotrac is the confirmed official source. Bills' remaining cap space post-FA estimated at $20M based on Buffalo Rumblings/Yahoo cap analysis after all restructures and signings.
